entry.title: 震痕｜Vibration and Traces
entry.content:
2026

4m * 4m * 3m

五金零件、馬達、拾音器、板金、電子元件

Hardware parts, motors, audio pickups, sheet metal, electronic components

In 2011, during an American football game in Seattle, over 60,000 fans cheered and jumped so hard for a touchdown that a nearby seismograph detected a 30-second vibration. This event was named the "Beast Quake." It not only made the touchdown player, Marshawn Lynch, famous in earth science but also changed how we think about an "Artificial earthquake"—showing it doesn't just come from intentional destruction.

"Artificial earthquake" means a ground vibration caused by human activity rather than nature, usually from energy development or scientific tests. However, as the global population grows, unintentional artificial earthquakes are also appearing. Even our daily walking and jumping can accidentally create vibrations strong enough to be measured, or even cause real damage to our surroundings.

Vibration and Traces captures the subtle movements and footsteps we often ignore in daily life. By amplifying these tiny vibrations and noises through electronic circuits, the energy is turned into power for a wall-drilling machine. During the exhibition, this artwork gathers vibrations from the environment to leave permanent marks in the space, turning invisible human movements into visible physical damage.

2011年在西雅圖的一場美式足球賽中，六萬多的球迷因為達陣得分集體狂歡跳動，竟然讓設置在球場附近的地震儀偵測到了持續30秒的震動，此地震被稱為「野獸地震（Beast Quake）」。這個事件成功讓達陣的球員Marshawn Lynch在地球科學界留下一席之位，同時，對於「人造地震」一詞的認知也不再僅限於人類刻意為之的破壞行為。

「人造地震」意指由人類活動引起而非自然產生的地殼震動，常見於能源開發或科學實驗。隨著人類數量加增，非刻意產生的人造地震也開始形成。僅僅是人日常中的移動、跳躍也會在無意中產生足以成為地震的震動，甚至實質的對環境造成破壞。

作品《震痕》嘗試捕捉人們日常中容易忽略的行走與移動，將其引起的微小震動與噪音透過電路放大，轉化為鑽牆的機械裝置動力來源。本作品在展覽期間匯聚環境中的震動，於空間中留下不可逆的痕跡，將無形的人類行為震動，具象化為實質的環境破壞。

Vibration and Traces consists of 15 wall-drilling installations. Each one is equipped with a custom vibration sensor and an amplifier circuit. We use audio pickups as sensors, placing them flat against floors or walls where they can easily catch sounds and tiny vibrations from the environment.

When the sensor catches a vibration, the signal is sent to an operational amplifier (Op-amp) to be amplified and adjusted. This step sets a "trigger threshold" and filters out background noise. This ensures that even the quietest sounds are accurately captured without accidental triggers, allowing the signal to steadily power the motors.
The mechanical parts of the 15 installations are mainly made of metal and mounted on the wall. Each one uses a DC motor with a grinding tip. A built-in spring structure keeps pushing the grinding tip firmly against the wall. When the motor is triggered by a vibration signal and starts to spin, it slowly wears away at the wall, drilling a hole that keeps getting deeper.

The artwork is displayed in a dark room. When a vibration is detected, it not only triggers the drilling but also lights up the installation, creating feedback through sound, light, and shadow. At the same time, the dust left behind from the drilling naturally becomes part of the artwork's visual story.

作品中包含有十五座的鑽牆裝置，每座裝置皆搭配有自製的震動感測器及震動放大電路。作品中使用拾音器作為震動感測器，並將感測器平貼於地面、牆面等易於捕捉環境與空間聲音和震動訊號的位置。

拾音器所捕捉到的震動訊號將經由運算放大器（Op-amp）的放大處理與基準電壓調校（Reference Voltage Calibration）。此調校機制主要用於設定系統的觸發門檻（Threshold Setting）與訊號偏置調整（DC Bias Adjustment），一方面確保微弱的聲音訊號能被精準轉譯，另一方面藉由控制靈敏度，避免環境雜訊造成誤觸，使訊號能穩定且具層次地驅動帶有鑽頭的直流電機。

在機械結構方面，裝置以金屬為主要材質並裝設於牆面上，利用直流馬達搭配砂輪磨頭執行鑽孔動作。透過裝置內的彈簧結構，磨頭能持續受到推力並緊抵牆面，當直流電機受到震動訊號驅動並轉動時，便會在牆上緩慢的磨損出一個孔洞並持續加深。
作品展示在暗室中，當偵測到震動時，除了驅動裝置鑽牆外，裝置本身帶有燈光亦會亮起，形成帶有聲音與光影的震動回饋。同時，裝置在鑽牆過程中所產生的粉塵遺留，也自然地成為作品視覺與敘事的一部分。

home

entry.title: 無感滯留｜Insensible Retention
entry.content:
Just because we do not notice something does not mean it did not happen. It is like how we always live in humidity, temperature, and airflow—cycles of condensation, dripping, and evaporation keep happening at the edge of our senses. Through tools, we catch these weak feelings that our bodies miss, making these changes stay so we can see them.

Instead of relying on digital data, the artwork " Insensible Retention " uses a pure physical mechanism to catch these insensible states in the room. The piece uses thermoelectric cooling chips to create temperature differences on metal surfaces. This causes the tiny, unnoticeable moisture in the air to condense into visible water droplets where the cold and heat meet. Set up in a matrix arrangement, the metal plates reflect how water condensation varies across the space due to changes in the airflow.

" Insensible Retention " tries to gather these microscopic environmental changes that we normally cannot feel. As the water droplets form, condense, and finally evaporate, the invisible state of the air is briefly held still through the artwork, allowing our bodies to feel its presence.

未曾意識到的事物，不代表它未曾發生。如同人始終浸泡於濕度、溫度與氣流之中，一次次凝結、滴落，再蒸發，不斷在感知的邊界發生。我們藉由工具，攔截那些被身體忽略的微弱感知，使原本無感的變化得以滯留。

《無感滯留》透過純粹的物理機制，在空間中建立起對無感狀態的攔截。作品利用製冷晶片在金屬表面製造出的溫度差，使空氣中原本細小、難以感知的水氣，在冷熱交界處凝結成肉眼可見的水滴。透過金屬板的矩陣式排列，使空間中不同位置因氣流擾動所產生的凝水差異，得以反映在裝置表面，試圖將原本無法察覺的微小環境變化匯聚起來，隨著水滴的生成、凝結與最終的蒸發，無形的空氣狀態在作品的運作中獲得了短暫的停滯，並使身體得以感知。

home

entry.title: 感電｜In the Current
entry.content:
2026

3m * 3m * 3m

電器設備、電感元件、金屬、電子電路、喇叭

Electrical equipment, inductive components, metal, electronic circuits, speakers

Every creature experiences the world differently. For example, birds can see a wider range of colors than we can, and bats can hear much higher frequencies. While we feel absolutely nothing from the electromagnetic fields around us, to a bee, they are an invisible storm vibrating every hair on its body. To a shark, they feel like the heartbeat of an unseen prey.

Today, we have filled the sky and earth with invisible electrical currents and signals, yet we remain completely unaware of them. To other creatures, humans seem like strangers in a noisy world we built ourselves.

In an era hard to leave electronic products, people inevitably spend long periods immersed in various electromagnetic signals. These weak, continuous waves fill the air like a transparent background. People usually focus only on the content and function carried by the signals, rather than the signals themselves as an existing reality. These electromagnetic activities keep vibrating, overlapping, and flowing outside our sense of perception, creating a silent but non-stop noise.

In the Current uses coils to detect electromagnetic waves from everyday appliances and amplifies them into sounds we can actually hear. By interacting with the piece, you can play with these hidden sounds and create your own rhythms of light and audio, giving you a fresh look at the technological signals woven into our daily lives.

每種生物所能感受到的波都有所不同，譬如，人類能看見的光譜比鳥類要來的窄，能聽見的音域也比蝙蝠要來的低頻。人類對日常周遭的電磁場毫無感覺，但對於蜜蜂而言，那可能是全身毛髮都在隨之震顫的隱形風暴；對鯊魚而言，是一隻看不見的巨大獵物在不斷跳動。

當我們用無形的交流電與訊號填滿了整片天空與土地，自己卻一無所知、一無所感。在牠們眼裡，人類對這座由自己一手打造的喧囂世界，竟是如此陌生。

在一個難以離開電子產品的時代，人們無可避免地長時間浸身於各種電磁訊號之中。這些在空氣中瀰漫的微弱持續波動，卻多半被視為透明的背景，人們關注的往往只是訊號所承載的內容與功能，而非訊號本身作為一種正在發生的存在。這些電磁活動在人的感知範圍之外持續顫動、交疊與流動，形成一種無聲卻不間斷的喧囂。

作品《感電》透過線圈感測電器產生的電磁波，並將其放大為人耳可聽見的聲響。觀眾能透過作品操作，將這些日常物件的電磁波聲響組合成自由的聲光節奏，進而重新檢視這些遍布在我們日常生活中的科技訊號。

技術與設計說明 Technical and Design

When we were kids, adults always warned us: "Don't leave electronics by your bedside, or you won't sleep well." Inspired by this childhood memory, the artwork In the Current recreates a small bedside scene, using the artist's own everyday items like a nightlight, charger, alarm clock, fan, and power strip. By using coils and inductors, the piece captures the faint electromagnetic waves these objects emit while running. An operational amplifier (Op-amp) then boosts these signals into sounds we can actually hear, allowing us to explore the invisible waves behind our daily technology.

To make the sound more engaging, the artwork includes a custom-built sequencer. Instead of letting the electromagnetic waves play as continuous, monotonous noise, the sequencer turns them into musical notes and rhythms. People can freely adjust the speed and beats, which also changes the flashing patterns of the lights. This allows people to remix the hidden sounds of these bedside electronics into a fun, playful piece of music.

小時候大人總會叮嚀：不要把電子產品放在床頭，以免影響睡眠。作品《感電》延伸自這段童年記憶，收集了作者床頭的電子產品，包含夜燈、充電器、鬧鐘、風扇與延長線等，將其布置成一個小型的床頭場景於展覽現場。作品透過線圈與電感，感測這些物件運作時散發的微弱電磁波，再藉由運算放大器（Op-amp）將訊號放大至人耳可聽見的聲頻範圍，藉此探索日常科技背後的無形訊號。

同時，作品結合了自製的序列器（Sequencer），使電感捕捉到的電磁聲響不再只是持續播放的單調噪音，而是能被視為一種聲音元素。透過序列器編排出的各式節奏，觀眾可以自由操控節拍的增減與速度，同時也觸發燈光的閃爍頻率，將這些床頭電子產品的電磁波聲響，重組為充滿趣味的音樂。

home

entry.title: 那些文字已經無關緊要了｜Those words are no longer relevant
entry.content:
2026

5m * 5m * 3m

手機、網路、控制電路系統、五金零件、馬達

Mobile phones, internet, control circuit systems, hardware parts, motors

語言透過多元字詞形構語意；然而，在當前數位時代，關鍵字改變了語言的結構，龐大的資料庫在經過層層演算後，化為單詞，使得有些文字在此「關鍵字時代」下被定義成無關緊要，在演算法中被抹去其存在的必要性。串聯起句意的字詞正在喪失，一次次的演算減輕了資訊、改變了語言，而我們自身也正在無感中減輕，越來越輕。

Statement Languages convey meanings through diverse words and phrases. In the digital age, however keywords have altered linguistic structures. Large databases have been reduced to words through layers of algorithms, rendering some words irrelevant and unnecessary to exist in the “keyword era.” Words that make sentences meaningful are disappearing. Repeated computing simplifies information and changes languages, and we are becoming human flotsam with our utter indifference.

home

entry.title: Leashing the Socket
entry.content:
Sockets are all around us in our daily lives, forming a network of electricity that outlines the boundaries of human existence and distinguishes the activities of "modern people." This relationship between humans and sockets creates an unseen force that attaches and restricts us.

Action

We tries to swap the roles of supply and demand between sockets and mobile phones. By having the mobile phone supply power to the socket, it comes alive and starts moving around. The connection through the power cord ties humans and sockets together, resulting in a humorous scenario of "Leash- ing pets." This also invites the audience to ponder: Who leashes whom? Who truly holds the power?

After reversing this dynamic, the intention is to reveal the intricate network formed by humans and non-human elements. The aim is to magnify and liberate it, allowing it to exist as an inde- pendent entity. This introduces the act of "walk- ing" into the relationship between sockets and people, underscoring the unequal state between them. As we walk and follow each other, we continually break free from the confines of the current network, striving to build autonomous individuals.

Next?

By flipping the relationship between subject and object, will the resources inherently possessed by humans be set free?After we stole the power of the audience's mobile phone, we stored the remaining power. These batteries, powered by the audience, will go to the next place....

Design Socket
Donate socket
Design Charger

home

entry.title: 900MHz
entry.summary: 900MHz 語言、文字、符號，人類溝通的媒介，如何突破七段顯示器的框架傳遞訊息？ 在一字一句都需要計價的傳訊時代，訊息內容不斷精煉演化，從數字延伸到英文字母，又或者延伸至以讀音的諧音傳達訊息，甚至當語言、文字無法表達時，改以表情符號、圖文示意。…
entry.content:
900MHz

語言、文字、符號，人類溝通的媒介，如何突破七段顯示器的框架傳遞訊息？
在一字一句都需要計價的傳訊時代，訊息內容不斷精煉演化，從數字延伸到英文字母，又或者延伸至以讀音的諧音傳達訊息，甚至當語言、文字無法表達時，改以表情符號、圖文示意。 BB Call的年代，我們以900MHz頻率聯繫彼此，創造出特有的傳訊文化。如今溝通工具已大幅減低人們傳訊的門檻，傳訊文化似乎並未隨之消逝，隱藏在我們已習以為常的溝通中。

Do you remember the days of the pager?

This 900MHz device let us stay connected to each other. The numbers on the screen not only conveyed simple messages, but also created a unique communication culture.Todays, staying in touch is easier, but no longer precious. We hope to bring you back to the 1990s, inviting you to relive the warmth of those numeric messages.

As the digits flash on the screen, you’re invited to experience the joy of hidden codes and feel the emotion behind each message once again.

- Website Link -

共同創作者：林芸均、 陳懿、陳苡瑄、蔡承嶧、戴婕茵
展覽：2025 台南 月津港燈節 - 月津超新星燈區：前望的回顧
特別感謝：nthu.maker.lab

home

entry.title: 浮海 ｜Pulsation Sea
entry.summary: 此時，人們正經歷海平面每年上升3到4毫米的階段，隨著氣候變遷將遽增為每年4到9毫米。海平面的上升並非只是土地的消逝，更影響到人與環境的共處關係。《浮海》這件作品試圖放大人為干涉環境所造成的劇烈連鎖狀態，透過「蛇擺」曳引的視覺效果去重現人與自然時而「同步」時而「混沌」的牽制狀態，進而思考兩者間該如何共處，又該如何面對未來的異變。 Currently, sea…
entry.content:
< 2024 台南 月津港燈節-通往銀河的便利指南 >

< 台南 鹽水月之美術館典藏 >

此時，人們正經歷海平面每年上升3到4毫米的階段，隨著氣候變遷將遽增為每年4到9毫米。海平面的上升並非只是土地的消逝，更影響到人與環境的共處關係。《浮海》這件作品試圖放大人為干涉環境所造成的劇烈連鎖狀態，透過「蛇擺」曳引的視覺效果去重現人與自然時而「同步」時而「混沌」的牽制狀態，進而思考兩者間該如何共處，又該如何面對未來的異變。

Currently, sea levels rise annually at 3 to 4 millimeters, with predictions of an acceleration to 4 to 9 millimeters per year due to climate change. Beyond land loss, these rising sea levels significantly affect the human-environment relationship. “Pulsation Sea” magnifies the profound chain reactions resulting from human environmental interventions. Through a “Pendulum Wave” visual effect, it portrays the dynamic connection between humans and nature, oscillating between “synchronization” and “chaos”, stimulating reflection on coexistence and facing future uncertainties.


展覽：2024 台南 月津港燈節-通往銀河的便利指南
進場協力：詹登凱、蔡承嶧
特別感謝：數位美學暨科技文化研究實驗室

home

entry.title: 數位循環｜Mass Cycles
entry.summary: 現實環境正不斷轉換為資訊，使人們試圖去尋找能證明「資訊」存在的方法，當我們所認為虛擬的事物確實存在著，環境的變遷、水文的循環將因數位化而改變，數據統計、數位化保存等亦改變了人們認知的自然，我們所認為的真實漸漸從虛擬中得知，使現實蒸發為虛擬又凝結為真實。然而數位與虛擬納為自然的循環中，我們喪失了何物？在一次次數位的循環後又將遺忘哪些本質的意義？數位循環｜ The transformation…
entry.content:
< 2024 新北市美展 - 金獎 >

現實環境正不斷轉換為資訊，使人們試圖去尋找能證明「資訊」存在的方法，當我們所認為虛擬的事物確實存在著，環境的變遷、水文的循環將因數位化而改變，數據統計、數位化保存等亦改變了人們認知的自然，我們所認為的真實漸漸從虛擬中得知，使現實蒸發為虛擬又凝結為真實。然而數位與虛擬納為自然的循環中，我們喪失了何物？在一次次數位的循環後又將遺忘哪些本質的意義？數位循環｜

The transformation of our physical environment into information makes people seek ways to validate "information's" existence. As what we perceive as virtual elements indeed exists, shift natural cycles through digitization, altering our grasp of the natural world. Reality unfolds from the virtual, evaporating into the digital and solidifying back into reality. Amidst this digital-virtual cycle, what's lost? Which essential meanings fade after repeated digital shifts?

「數位循環」嘗試將虛擬地景介入現實環境當中，藉此捕捉數位循環中所喪失的「資訊的重量」。透過實地掃描自然景觀後，運用點雲技術複製出虛擬地景，計算其資訊位元的重量並與現實地景想比對，最後將消逝的重量透過霧化裝置噴出等重量的水霧消散至空間中，正如那些數位化後所蒸發至無的現實樣貌，龐大且充斥在周遭，正在消逝卻無人察覺。

"Mess Cycle" introduces virtual landscapes to physical environments, capturing lost "information weight." Scanning natural scenes and recreating virtual ones using point cloud tech, we calculate their informational weight and compare with real surroundings. The vanishing weight disperses through misting, paralleling the evaporation of digitized realities. Imperceptible yet vast, these shifts engulf us, fading away undetected.

home

entry.title: 浮點數｜Float
entry.summary: 現實世界中的行為與物件正不斷轉換成為大量的「資訊」，當人們試圖尋 找能證明「資訊」存在的方法時，也開始嘗試以科學方法計算出「資訊重 量」。然而，成為資訊態的微小物質重量，終究與現實中人們所感受到物…
entry.content:
現實世界中的行為與物件正不斷轉換成為大量的「資訊」，當人們試圖尋 找能證明「資訊」存在的方法時，也開始嘗試以科學方法計算出「資訊重 量」。然而，成為資訊態的微小物質重量，終究與現實中人們所感受到物 體的有著極大差異。我們進一步去思考:數位技術在壓縮、消除人們印象 中的實體之際，世界存在的重量也正快速被濃縮、損耗、蒸散，而我們卻 毫無覺察。

In the real world, actions and objects are continuously being transformed into information. As people attempt to find ways to prove the existence of information, they also start to explore the calculation of its "weight" using scientific methods. However, the weight of tiny particles in their informational state differs greatly from the perceived weight of physical objects in reality. This prompts us to consider further:
as digital technology compresses and erases our impression of physicality, the weight of existence in the world is rapidly condensed, depleted, and dissipated, all
while we remain unaware.

「浮點數」試圖探究在數位技術中經過層層轉換被蒸散掉的重量究竟 為何?作品蒐集關於「I lost ......(我失去......)」的訊息，關注每一個物質 性的消逝。每一次單位裝置的運行，即代表著一條資訊訊息的產製、傳遞 與消散。在數位化過程中遺失重量後，僅存的微小重量藉由裝置將其蒸散 於場域之中，試圖找回人們對於「失去」的身體感受。

"Float" seeks to explore the weight that dissipates through successive transformations within digital technology. The artwork collects messages about "I lost..." and focuses on the materialistic disappearance of each instance. The operation of each unit device represents the generation, transmission, and dissipation of an information message. After losing weight in the process of digitization, the remaining minuscule weight is vaporized into the field through the devices, aiming to recover people's bodily perception of "loss".

home

entry.title: From Here to There
entry.summary: 兔子在神秘的洞口之間穿梭，探索護城河的景色，觀察河邊的人們，靈活俐落的動作，輕盈又優雅地完成每一次跳動。躍身與落下，乘載了一年跌宕裡的笑與淚，穿越了河道，步步通往新年的企盼。 「From Here…
entry.content:
兔子在神秘的洞口之間穿梭，探索護城河的景色，觀察河邊的人們，靈活俐落的動作，輕盈又優雅地完成每一次跳動。躍身與落下，乘載了一年跌宕裡的笑與淚，穿越了河道，步步通往新年的企盼。

「From Here 兔 There」，由河岸上觀眾的碰觸為起點，開啟兔子在河面上跳躍、蛻變的旅程，點亮城市的希望，從這裡到那裡，帶領人們迎向嶄新的一年。

共同製作：游覺輔、賴玟霓、劉怡萱、黃睿緯、林易萱、陳虹宇、程馨、謝長穎
展覽：2023.1「新竹過好年 萬兔順利Jump!」2023新竹燈節，新竹護城河，新竹
特別感謝：數位美學暨科技文化研究實驗室

home
